summary: Phase 4 retest. BACKEND: 7 passed + 2 skipped — 100% green (test_redeploy_webhook 3 passed, 2 skipped + test_phase4_user_stats 4 passed; happy-path NOT triggered). FRONTEND: BOTH bugs from iter_10 are STILL PRESENT — neither fix worked. (1) Login.js ?error=access_denied still does NOT show a toast: the [data-sonner-toaster] element is NOT in the DOM at all when /login first renders. The toaster ONLY appears AFTER the first toast call from a sibling user action (verified on /sms-login: clicking 'Envoyer' triggers toast.success and only THEN does <section aria-label='Notifications'> mount with the toast li). So toast.error fired in Login's useEffect on mount is dropped because Sonner's Toaster useEffect (which subscribes to the toast queue) hasn't run yet — it's a sibling in App.js wrapped under <AuthProvider>+<CacheProvider>+<LanguageProvider> and uses next-themes useTheme inside /components/ui/sonner.jsx, so it mounts AFTER the route's Login.useEffect. The new toastedRef + direct window.location.search read does NOT solve this — it's a render-order race, not a duplicate-firing race. (2) Onboarding joyride disableOverlay+spotlightClicks props do NOT remove the blocking overlay: the playwright click on user-menu-trigger gets intercepted by '<path fill="#00000080" d="M0 0H1920V1080H0Z"></path>' from <div id='react-joyride-portal'>. That's still the full-viewport overlay. So disableOverlay didn't take effect in react-joyride v3.0.2 — likely needs a different prop name in v3 or the build is using stale chunks.

ui_bugs.issue: [data-sonner-toaster] is absent from DOM on initial /login render — Sonner's Toaster element only mounts AFTER the first toast() call. Login.useEffect calls toast.error() before Sonner has subscribed, so it's silently dropped. CONFIRMED: navigated to /login?error=access_denied (and ?error=invalid_request, server_error, foo_unknown) — 0 toasts in DOM, no [data-sonner-toaster], 'Connexion refusée' nowhere on page. On /sms-login the same Toaster IS missing initially but appears after toast.success fires from button click. Real fix likely: remove the next-themes wrapper indirection in /components/ui/sonner.jsx (use sonner's <Toaster /> directly with theme='dark' literal), OR move the toast call to a defer microtask that waits for Sonner to subscribe (e.g. requestAnimationFrame chain or a small queueMicrotask + check that document.querySelector('[data-sonner-toaster]') exists, otherwise retry via MutationObserver), OR mount the Toaster ABOVE AuthProvider/CacheProvider/LanguageProvider so it initializes first.

disableOverlay={true} + spotlightClicks prop did NOT eliminate the full-viewport overlay. <path fill='#00000080' d='M0 0H1920V1080H0Z'> still renders inside #react-joyride-portal and intercepts clicks on [data-testid=user-menu-trigger]. Playwright timed out clicking it (overlay path catches pointer events). Likely the prop in react-joyride v3 changed name OR the Joyride component still renders the overlay path despite disableOverlay (it might be specifically the Floater backdrop, not the overlay). Try `styles={{ overlay: { display: 'none' }, spotlight: {...} }}` OR `hideOverlay` (v3 alt name) OR set `disableScrolling` + lower zIndex. Also verify via `document.querySelector('#react-joyride-portal path')` after rendering with disableOverlay.

action_items: URGENT: Fix Sonner Toaster mounting — root cause is render order. The Toaster element is NOT rendered until first toast() fires. Two practical fixes: (a) replace /components/ui/sonner.jsx wrapper with a direct `<Toaster theme='dark' position='top-right' />` (remove next-themes useTheme — it likely causes Toaster's mount effect to be deferred / skipped on first paint when no ThemeProvider is present), AND (b) hoist <Toaster> ABOVE <AuthProvider>/<CacheProvider>/<LanguageProvider> in App.js so its useEffect (which subscribes to ToastState) runs BEFORE Login's useEffect.

URGENT: Fix joyride overlay — disableOverlay+spotlightClicks didn't work in v3.0.2. Use `styles={{ overlay: { pointerEvents: 'none', display: 'none' } }}` OR check the v3 prop list (could be `hideOverlay` or `disableOverlayClose`+manual style override). Verify by checking `document.querySelector('#react-joyride-portal svg path')` returns null after the change.

After both fixes land: re-run /login?error=access_denied (toast must show 'Connexion refusée') AND fresh-SMS-login → click [data-testid=user-menu-trigger] (must open menu while joyride spotlight is on wizard-btn).
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/components/ui/sonner.jsx: wrapper imports `useTheme` from next-themes — there is no <ThemeProvider> ancestor in App.js, so useTheme returns the default empty context. While the destructure `const { theme = 'system' } = useTheme()` doesn't throw (because next-themes context default is {}, not undefined), the wrapper still adds an unnecessary indirection that delays Toaster's mount. Recommend deleting the wrapper and importing { Toaster } directly from 'sonner' in App.js.

/app/frontend/src/pages/Login.js useEffect runs SYNCHRONOUSLY after first paint of <Login>, which happens BEFORE the sibling <Toaster> in App.js completes its own useEffect (subscribe to sonner's ToastState). React commits effects bottom-up but for siblings it's source-order — Login is rendered as the matched <Route element>, so its child-effects fire before App's wrapper-level Toaster effect. Hence the toast event is fired into a queue with no subscribers and dropped (sonner v2 doesn't replay events to late subscribers).
react-joyride v3 changed several prop names from v2; please check the v3 changelog before assuming `disableOverlay` works as documented in the v2 README.

success_rate.frontend: ~50% — backend integration OK (SMS login → dashboard, /user/stats, EmptyProjectsState all work), but BOTH iter_10 bugs remain UNFIXED

test_credentials: /app/memory/test_credentials.md current. SMS demo confirmed (codes returned in API response, also displayed in UI). DEPLOY_SECRET 401 case verified, happy path NOT triggered.
seed_data_creation: Created several SMS demo users (+33612345999, +33612003393, +33612006513) via send/verify flow. Each generates one row in db.users + db.user_sessions. Safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 retest = FAIL on the two specific fixes. Backend perfect. Frontend: (1) The fundamental Toaster bug is that [data-sonner-toaster] isn't in DOM on /login first paint — verify with: page.evaluate('!!document.querySelector("[data-sonner-toaster]")') AFTER networkidle+3s wait — should be true (currently false). The toaster only appears once toast() fires for the first time. (2) The joyride overlay still has a fullscreen <path fill='#00000080' d='M0 0H1920V1080H0Z'> blocking clicks — even with disableOverlay+spotlightClicks. Verify with: document.querySelector('#react-joyride-portal path[fill="#00000080"]') — should be null after fix.
rca of the issue: Bug 1 (Login toast): Sonner's <Toaster /> uses an internal subscription pattern. When toast() is called, sonner's toastState dispatches to ALL currently-subscribed Toasters. Login.js useEffect runs at first paint of the matched route — but the App-level Toaster (wrapped under multiple Providers + uses next-themes useTheme indirection) hasn't mounted/subscribed yet. The toast dispatch goes into the void. The previous fix (toastedRef + window.location.search) only addressed double-firing in StrictMode, not the render-order race. Fix is to delay the toast.error to AFTER Toaster has subscribed (e.g. requestAnimationFrame + check [data-sonner-toaster] exists) OR hoist Toaster above all providers and remove the next-themes wrapper. Bug 2 (joyride overlay): react-joyride v3.0.2's disableOverlay prop does NOT have the documented effect for this app. The fullscreen black path still renders inside #react-joyride-portal. Need to either inline-style the overlay to display:none via the styles prop, or upgrade/downgrade joyride, or use a different overlay-disable mechanism in v3.
